title: Today's AI Brief: Strengthening societal resilience with Rosalind Biodefense
summary: OpenAI launches Rosalind Biodefense, Google I/O 2026 Dialogues stage highlights

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. Today, we're looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. OpenAI has just launched Rosalind Biodefense, a new initiative that's all about using AI to improve biodefense, public health, and pandemic preparedness. I'm intrigued by how they're expanding access to their GPT-Rosalind model for vetted developers and U.S. government partners.

OpenAI's Rosalind Biodefense is a big deal. By working with trusted partners, they're aiming to harness the power of frontier AI to tackle some of the world's most pressing health challenges. It's a great example of how AI can be used for good, and I'm excited to see where this goes.

Also making waves is Google's I/O 2026 Dialogues stage, which brought together some of the brightest minds in tech to discuss the latest breakthroughs in AI. From what I've seen, the highlights are pretty fascinating – everything from advancements in model economics to new approaches to data boundaries and safety.

What stands out to me is the emphasis on trust and distribution. As AI becomes more pervasive, it's clear that we need to be thinking carefully about how these models are developed, deployed, and used. It's not just about building cool tech; it's about building tech that benefits society as a whole.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: Strengthening societal resilience with Rosalind Biodefense
